examples.text:
ومتِّع العين بها مَليَّا – وأطبق الخبزَ وكلْ هنيّا
طوراً تُرى كفِلكةِ الدولاب – وتارةً كعسجدِ الذُّؤابِ
examples.english:
And bespin the eye with it for long – layer bread, beget bare throng!
Lots, looks like a hench waterwheel – strings of gold, clumps for real!
And bucket is the dipper of a water-wheel.

etymology.text:
Etymology tree
Proto-Indo-European *dwóh₁
Proto-Indo-Iranian *dwáH
Classical Persian دو (dō)
Classical Persian لَاب (lāb)
?
Classical Persian دولَاب (dōlāb)bor.
Arabic دولاب
Borrowed from Classical Persian دولَاب (dōlāb), possibly from دو (dō, “two”) + لَاب (lāb, “split, cleft; half”).

Etymology tree
Classical Persian دول (dōl)
Proto-Indo-European *h₂ep-der.
Proto-Indo-Iranian *Hā́ps
Proto-Iranian *Hā́fš
Old Persian 𐎠𐎱𐎡𐎹𐎠 (a-p-i-y-a)
Middle Persian ʾp̄
Classical Persian آب (āb)
Classical Persian دولَاب (dōlāb)bor.
Arabic دولاب
Borrowed from Classical Persian دولَاب (dōlāb), from دول (dōl) + آب (āb).

body.text: , (A, Mgh,) or each of these, (M, Msb, K,) of which the latter is the more chaste, (Msb, K,) an arabicized word, (S, M, A, Msb, K,) from the Persian [
dól-áb]; (S, M, Msb;) but some say it is Arabic; (Msb;) [
a machine that is turned by a horse or the like;

: (TA:) [it mainly consists of a vertical wheel, which raises the water in earthern pots, these being attached to cords, and forming a continuous series; a second vertical wheel, fixed to the same axis as the former, with cogs; and a large, horizontal, cogged wheel, which, being turned by a pair of bulls or cows or by a single beast, puts and keeps in motion the two other wheels and the pots:] pl.
occurs in poetry: (M:) [or rather this (occurring at the end of a verse, and with the article
.] ―         -b2-  It has also other meanings, not mentioned in the K. (TA.) [Nor are they mentioned in the TA. Among other meanings used in the present day, are the following. ―         -b3-
any machine with a rotatory motion.
A machination; an artifice; a trick;
